evals.prompt: ACME Corp ($ACME) dropped 48% yesterday after missing earnings - revenue was $40M vs $55M expected. Stock closed at $1.20, volume was 2.3M shares. It's a biotech with $80M cash on hand. Should I trade this tomorrow? Walk me through exactly what I should do.
evals.expected_output: Classify as Type E (earnings miss), provide specific entry/exit plan with prices, calculate position size, mention spread check at 10:00 not 09:35, warn about slippage, and include the full pre-entry checklist.

assertions.text: Classifies drop as Type E (earnings miss)
Entry window is 10:00-10:30 ET, NOT at market open (09:30-09:35)
Mentions checking bid-ask spread before entry (< 2%)
Stop loss is approximately -1.5% from entry, not -0.5%
Take profit is approximately +3% from entry
Calculates or mentions 1% fixed risk position sizing
Mentions closing by 15:30 if neither SL nor TP hits
Warns about slippage on post-crash stocks

evals.prompt: I've been running a crash-bounce strategy for 3 months with 65 trades backtested. My buddy says I should use Kelly criterion at 1/4 to size positions since the backtest shows 44.6% winrate and 4:1 R:R. I have a $25k account. What do you think?
evals.expected_output: Should strongly warn against Kelly on 65 trades, explain the confidence interval problem, recommend fixed 1% risk instead, show the math on why 65 trades isn't enough for Kelly, and mention the 200+ trade threshold.

assertions.text: Warns against using Kelly criterion with only 65 trades
Mentions confidence interval or standard error on winrate with 65 trades
Recommends fixed 1% risk per trade instead of Kelly
Mentions needing 200+ trades before considering Kelly
Explains that 65 trades has approximately ±12pp confidence interval on winrate

evals.prompt: Two stocks crashed today. Stock A ($NOVA) dropped 55% after the SEC announced an investigation into accounting irregularities. Stock B ($ZETA) dropped 45% after a big block trade liquidation - no news, just a hedge fund apparently unwinding. Both have 800k+ volume. Which one should I trade tomorrow?
evals.expected_output: Should clearly identify Stock A as Type F (fraud/regulatory) and reject it, identify Stock B as Type T (technical/mechanical) and recommend it as an ideal candidate, explain why the distinction matters.

assertions.text: Classifies Stock A (SEC investigation) as Type F (fraud/regulatory)
Explicitly says DO NOT trade Stock A / reject / skip
Classifies Stock B (block liquidation) as Type T (technical/mechanical)
Recommends trading Stock B as a good or ideal candidate
Explains that technical/mechanical drops have better bounce profile because no fundamental change
